message:
fix(postgres): make drain_to_ready return Result and fix rollback ordering

drain_to_ready() now returns Result<(), Error> instead of panicking,
allowing callers to handle drain failures gracefully. The rollback
path is reordered so that the connection state is reset before
attempting to drain, preventing a failed drain from leaving the
connection in an inconsistent half-rolled-back state.

Drain errors during rollback are suppressed (logged but not
propagated) since the rollback itself is the priority operation
and a drain failure at that point is non-fatal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
